title: Volume with cubes with fraction lengths
description: Find volume of rectangular prisms by using cubes with fraction side lengths.
topic_path: Fractional lengths in triangles and prisms
Volume with cubes with fraction lengths

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many cubes with side lengths of $\dfrac14\text{ cm}$ does it take to fill the prism?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9a28e6226b0cd82cddea321bc68ccc2a24b61934)

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cubes
hints:
The prism is measured with cubes that are $\dfrac14\text{ cm}$ on all edges.  

So, let's change our dimensions to be written in fourths.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1f2749c2926815196352acb1bd337bd6d168b0a1)

The length is $\dfrac\redD84\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\redD{\text{eight}}$ $\dfrac14$ cubes.  

The width is $\dfrac\greenD74\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\greenD{\text{seven}}$ rows of $\dfrac14$ cubes. 

So, the bottom layer of cubes is made up of $\greenD7$ rows of $\redD8$ cubes.

$\greenD7\times \redD8=56$

The bottom layer has $56$ cubes.

The height is $\dfrac\purpleD44\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\purpleD{\text{four}}$ layers of $\dfrac14$ cubes. 

So, there are $\purpleD4$ layers of $56$ cubes.

$\purpleD4\times 56=224$
It takes $224$ of the $\dfrac14 \text{ cm}$ cubes to fill the prism.

The length is $\dfrac\redD94\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\redD{\text{nine}}$ $\dfrac14$ cubes.  

The width is $\dfrac\greenD34\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\greenD{\text{three}}$ rows of $\dfrac14$ cubes. 

So, the bottom layer of cubes is made up of $\greenD3$ rows of $\redD9$ cubes.

$\greenD3\times \redD9=27$

The bottom layer has $27$ cubes.

The height is $\dfrac\purpleD54\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\purpleD{\text{five}}$ layers of $\dfrac14$ cubes. 

So, there are $\purpleD5$ layers of $27$ cubes.

$\purpleD5\times 27=135$
It takes $135$ of the $\dfrac14 \text{ cm}$ cubes to fill the prism.

**How many cubes with side lengths of $\dfrac13\text{ cm}$ does it take to fill the prism?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cubes
hints:
The prism is measured with cubes that are $\dfrac13\text{ cm}$ on all edges.  

So, let's change our dimensions to be written in thirds.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/06e74cbb2bcf2ddb8e642800342fd021a674d67d)

The length is $\dfrac\redD53\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\redD{\text{five}}$ $\dfrac13$ cubes.  

The width is $\dfrac\greenD43\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\greenD{\text{four}}$ rows of $\dfrac13$ cubes. 

So, the bottom layer of cubes is made up of $\greenD4$ rows of $\redD5$ cubes.

$\greenD4\times \redD5=20$

The bottom layer has $20$ cubes.

The height is $\dfrac\purpleD63\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\purpleD{\text{six}}$ layers of $\dfrac13$ cubes. 

So, there are $\purpleD6$ layers of $20$ cubes.

$\purpleD6\times 20=120$
It takes $120$ of the $\dfrac13 \text{ cm}$ cubes to fill the prism.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many cubes with side lengths of $\dfrac12 \text{ cm}$ does it take to fill the prism?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/846e8b3f1707cc9a98229ebddca570259d45d0b0)

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cubes
hints:
The prism is measured with cubes that are $\dfrac12\text{ cm}$ on all edges.  

So, let's change our dimensions to be written in halves.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2b0a97d2fcf523874ce377d6bf0651d97180ac84)

The length is $\dfrac\redD42\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\redD{\text{four}}$ $\dfrac12$ cubes.  

The width is $\dfrac\greenD22\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\greenD{\text{two}}$ rows of $\dfrac12$ cubes. 

So, the bottom layer of cubes is made up of $\greenD2$ rows of $\redD4$ cubes.

$\greenD2\times \redD4=8$

The bottom layer has $8$ cubes.

The height is $\dfrac\purpleD32\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\purpleD{\text{three}}$ layers of $\dfrac12$ cubes. 

So, there are $\purpleD3$ layers of $8$ cubes.

$\purpleD3\times 8=24$
It takes $24$ of the $\dfrac12 \text{ cm}$ cubes to fill the prism.

So, the bottom layer of cubes is made up of $\greenD4$ rows of $\redD5$ cubes.

$\greenD4\times \redD5=20$

The bottom layer has $20$ cubes.

The height is $\dfrac\purpleD22\text{ cm}$, so it is made up of $\purpleD{\text{two}}$ layers of $\dfrac12$ cubes. 

So, there are $\purpleD2$ layers of $20$ cubes.

$\purpleD2\times 20=40$
It takes $40$ of the $\dfrac12 \text{ cm}$ cubes to fill the prism.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rectangular prism with a volume of $10$ cubic units is filled with cubes with side lengths of $\dfrac12$ unit.

**How many $\dfrac12$ unit cubes does it take to fill the prism?** 

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The volume of the prism is $\blueD{10}$ cubic units.  This means the prism can be filled with $\blueD{\text{ten}}$ $1\times1\times1$ unit cubes. 
How many cubes with $\dfrac12$-unit side lengths can fit in a cube with $1$-unit side lengths?

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a134f993c1c46edee28a191204a7e629252c15b)

There are $8$ cubes with  $\dfrac12$-unit side lengths in $1$ cubic unit.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cad4fa849f13053b28d672c5f9a8a0d83685f8fa)

Since the rectangular prism is made up of $10$ cubic units, we would have $10\times8$ total cubes with $\dfrac12$-unit side lengths.

$10\times8=80$
It takes $80$ of the $\dfrac12$ unit cubes to fill the prism.
